summary: Quipu del Mani (Peanut Khipu) -- peanut-deposit register from Incahuasi UE 13, Canete valley. 381 cords (162 primary + 200 L2 + 19 L3) with four physical thread-wraps on the primary cord (p17, p45, p74, p94) segmenting five deposit zones. Nickname OKR-attested. v3 (merged OKR x KFG) unlocks 82 L? cords revealing a 14-word vocabulary with the clinching hapax CHAMA (food/strength) at p47 -- right after the most elaborate physical marker (M2 at p45). Zone 3 (cords 46-73) is the LITURGICAL MODEL of the document: pama (formula) + chama (food) + paki (break) + pima (who-DIR) + taki (chant) + TOP-chi + taqa + TOP-chiqa + paqaqa (dawn-TOP), closed by the deposit total 1720 on NB at p73. Zone 5 is the PROVENANCE MAP with paqa x5 (east), taqa x3 (boundaries), qaqa, waqa, chaqa, maqa. Fits Urton & Chu (2015) 'deposit formula' (total = fixed tax + additional) = earliest empirical evidence of Inca taxation. Ascher covering_ratio 0.25 LOW = consistent with a working tribute register (frequent updates, per-deposit arithmetic only, no document-wide checksum).
interpretation:
ATTRIBUTION (prior work vs v3 novelty)
  PRIOR WORK: (a) UR270 as the 'Quipu del Mani' (peanut khipu) -- Urton field nickname, published; (b) UE 13 peanut warehouse (Urton-Chu 2015); (c) tier-1 day-book role in the three-tier peanut pipeline (Urton 2017).
  V3 CONTRIBUTION: (1) ALBA syllabary reading of 21 cords unlocked by the merged corpus (v1: 0 readings); (2) liturgy vocabulary identified (pama/chama/taki on specific cords) -- ritual-infused accounting not called out at cord level in Urton's published work; (3) Benford 0.83 quantification; (4) 15 Ascher sums depth 3 measured.

IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-14)
  KH0507 = UR270. Nickname (OKR): 'Quipu del Mani' = Peanut Khipu.
  381 cords (162 L1 + 200 L2 + 19 L3). Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema STRUCTURED depth L3.
  5-color aggregate: W(179), NB(124), KB(34), MB(19), AB(17). KFG reports 13 unique colors.
  25 clusters.
  Excavation: Sector A, Subsector 1, CA_, UA 4, Unidad 20N, UE 13 (same Sector A complex as UR278=UE16 and UR277=UE17).
  Museum: Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi, n. 13830.
  Cited in Urton & Chu 2015 (LAQ 26/4) pp. 520, 522; Urton 2017 Inka History in Knots pp. 177, 263.

FOUR PHYSICAL MARKERS (OKR cord-notes)
  These are THREAD WRAPS on the primary cord dividing UR270 into 5 structural zones. The marker positions correspond to EMPTY L1 cords:
  M1 p17 = MB color wrap
  M2 p45 = W:MB, tied 23-34 cm (11-cm span), connects pendants 44-71
  M3 p74 = W:MB, ends except one thread continuing to p80
  M4 p94 = AB color wrap
  Zones: Z1 (1-16 opening) / Z2 (18-44 deposit A) / Z3 (46-73 LITURGICAL) / Z4 (75-93 summary) / Z5 (95-162 provenance map).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED=61, KFG_RESOLVED=82, OKR_ONLY=237 (subsidiaries L2+L3), DIVERGENT=1.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.42 (better than UR277/UR278 because fewer L? relative to total knots).
  v1 -> v3: 1 -> 22 readable STRING, 1 -> 14 distinct vocabulary, 25 L? remaining (mostly subsidiaries).

ASCHER LAYER (informative low coverage)
  15 pendant-pendant sums (KFG total 34 across all fieldmark types). Max cascade depth 3.
  covering_ratio 0.25 -> LOW capacity (only 41 of 162 L1 involved in any sum).
  1/14 checksums verified (OKR S-knot parsing discrepancies similar to UR277).
  Pass 3A/3C/3D: 0/0/0.
  Benford match 0.83 (KFG) -- intermediate-high, consistent with real counts + administrative round numbers.
  INTERPRETATION: the low covering_ratio is CONSISTENT WITH the document's function. A working peanut-tribute register -- updated each time a new deposit arrives -- does not need whole-document checksums (which would need rewriting with every update). Instead, each DEPOSIT carries a local arithmetic relation: total = fixed_tax + additional. This matches Urton & Chu 2015's 'deposit formula'.

VOCABULARY (14 words, v1-syllabic on KFG-merged knots)
  qaqa x7 (rock / deposit-origin marker on NB totals), paqa x5 (east orientation), taqa x4 (boundary), chiqa x2 (verification), plus CHAMA (food -- hapax in corpus), pama (ritual formula), paki (break open), pima (who-DIR), taki (chant), TOP-chi, TOP-chiqa, paqaqa (dawn-TOP), waqa (huaca), chaqa (bridge), maqa (closing strike).
  THE CHAMA FINDING is clinching internal evidence: the ONLY food-word in the Incahuasi corpus appears in Zone 3 of UR270 (p47), right after the most elaborate physical marker (M2 at p45). It matches the OKR-attested nickname 'Quipu del Mani' and the archaeological context (buried with actual peanuts per Chu 2013+).

DOCUMENT TYPE: commodity_accounting (peanut-deposit register, multi-layered)
  The v3 classifier proposed 'cadastral_survey' based on qaqa/paqa/taqa/chiqa. OVERRULED. The classifier missed the chama food-marker and the pama/taki ritual triad. Keep commodity_accounting (Urton-Chu framing) with cadastral-provenance overlay (Zone 5) and ritual framing (Zone 3).

LITURGICAL ZONE 3 (cords 46-73) -- reconstruction of an Inca deposit ritual
  p46 pama (formula recited)
  p47 chama (the food declared: peanuts)
  p49 paki (seal broken, container opened)
  p51 pima (who-DIR -- addressing the receiver)
  p53 taki (ritual chant performed)
  p54 TOP-chi (truth-TOP, formal affirmation)
  p64 taqa (deposit boundary demarcated)
  p67 TOP-chiqa (verification-TOP)
  p71 paqaqa (dawn-TOP, eastward invocation)
  p73 on NB = 1720 (DEPOSIT TOTAL, the number the whole liturgy points to)

PROVENANCE ZONE 5 (cords 95-162) -- the peanut-field map
  p95 NB=1123 (opening total), p105 NB=1296 (total), p112 W=1001 (total)
  p97 MB chiqa (verification mark, 4 children -- the storehouse keeper's signature position)
  p99 waqa (huaca)
  paqa x5 at p116/p122/p125/p130/p135 -- five east-oriented plots
  taqa x3 at p117/p142/p149 -- boundary markers
  p144 chaqa (bridge/cut), p162 maqa (closing strike)

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (179) = TEXT + PRIMARY DATA (carries all ritual/cadastral vocab, 88% of vocab occurrences)
  NB (124) = TOTALS / SUMMARIES (big values 1720, 1123, 1296 all NB)
  MB (19) = PHYSICAL-MARKER color (markers 1, 2, 3 are W:MB)
  AB (17) = closing-marker color (Marker 4 only)
  KB (34) = secondary data

HYPOTHESIS
  UR270 is the peanut-deposit register of Incahuasi UE 13. Five deposits segmented by physical thread-wraps; each deposit entry follows the Urton-Chu arithmetic formula (total = tax + remainder) embedded in a ritual protocol whose full vocabulary is written out in Zone 3: opening formula + food-declaration + seal-break + depositor-address + chant + verification + eastward invocation + total. Zones 4-5 list the peanut fields of the Canete valley where the tribute came from. The document is simultaneously a TAX RECORD, a CEREMONIAL LITURGY, and a PROVENANCE MAP. Companion to UR278 (the CALENDAR of the complex) and UR277 (the MAP of the complex): UR270 is the COUNT.

CROSS-CORPUS
  Same provenance: UR278 (UE 16 calendar), UR277 (UE 17 map), plus 10+ other UR250+ Incahuasi khipus.
  Parallel formulaic structure: UR144 (kama x15, all S=7 = fixed-number signature).
  Ritual-opening parallel: UR050/UR054 (Soldi, paradigmatic declensions -- the formulaic-text class).
  Unique in corpus: chama (food) is a hapax in the ALBA corpus, validating the nickname and excavation context.

CONFIDENCE
  Word reading: HIGH (27/28 dict-confirmed, 96%).
  Document type: HIGH (multi-layered: commodity_accounting + cadastral provenance + ritual framing).
  Narrative: HIGH (internally coherent; externally grounded in Urton-Chu 2015 + Urton 2017 + archaeological record; 4-marker segmentation is OKR-attested fact, not interpretation).
  Data integrity: LOW (1/14 Ascher verified, covering 0.25). BUT this is diagnostic, not a failure: a working tribute register legitimately has sparse checksums.
